judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent fetched all articles and computed the overall maximum description length without filtering to Sports (or categorizing articles as Sports), so its execute_python step selected "Making RSS Scale" instead of the sports-specific maximum ("The Rundown").
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent retrieved all articles and then used a simplistic keyword-based substring filter on title/description (execute_python) to identify "sports" articles, which misclassified a Microsoft article and led it to select the wrong longest-description title instead of "The Rundown".
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After retrieving all articles, the agent used execute_python to classify "sports" via a naive regex built from broad keywords (e.g., including "final") without word boundaries on title/description, causing misclassification and selecting "PeopleSoft awaits Delaware court ruling" as the longest "sports" article.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After retrieving all articles (query_db) and computing description lengths, the agent’s execute_python step used an overly broad keyword filter (including terms like “game”) to classify sports articles, misclassifying a tech article and selecting "'Trustworthiness' still a goal for Microsoft" instead of the true sports article “The Rundown.”
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After fetching all article titles and descriptions from the articles collection, the agent computed the maximum description length across all articles and returned its title, but never identified or filtered to sports articles, missing the required categorization step.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
